v3_upstream_status.fallback_baseline: Existing 20260626 L group round 3 prediction chain was reviewed as the current available baseline.

v3_contract_flags.detail: The reviewed baseline states that predicted lineups are not official and need T-75 recheck, but does not provide the v3 structured predicted_lineups package with XI, confidence, conflicts, source log and rerun triggers.

The baseline has xG, Poisson, odds and final probabilities, but does not expose the full v3 factor_inputs, weights, third_round_context_adjustment, simulated_group_outcomes and duplicate-counting audit.
Same-source totals are missing, and Panama vs England ordinary 1X2 is unavailable in the reviewed snapshot.

blocking_gates.blocking_effect: hold final model lock and all betting-style language
availability and rotation-sensitive probabilities require cap or rerun
cannot pass the latest single-match v3 template

top_concerns.concern: T-75 official lineups and final availability are missing; Ghana goalkeeper/Partey questions and Croatia veteran minutes load remain pre-match swing inputs.
Ghana can benefit from a draw while Croatia are more win-dependent; late tempo can flip around 55-75 minutes depending on the simultaneous England match.
Croatia ordinary 1X2 market at 55.3% no-vig is stronger than model raw 48.3%; public/experience premium or market motivation pricing must be separated from model evidence.
Croatia experience, midfield control, second attacks, first-round correction and market signal may be counted across strength, tactics, player state and market calibration.

upset_or_failure_scenarios: Ghana sit in a low block and accept draw utility, turning the game into a 0-0 or 1-1 path longer than the model's Croatia-win headline suggests.
Croatia push late and open transition space for Ghana counters or set-piece swings.
An early card or early Ghana goal breaks the low-event under baseline and makes the static Poisson tail too thin.
overconfidence_flags: Croatia win at 50.4% is not strong enough for one-sided language.
Top score 1-0 is only 16.31%, with 0-0 and 1-1 close behind.
The current baseline lacks v3 predicted_lineups and full factor-weight exposure.
market_flags: Ordinary 1X2 and handicap snapshots exist, but same-source totals are absent.
Croatia -1 handicap market points to handicap failure risk, not a clear-margin favorite profile.
Single capture at 2026-06-26T17:43:00+08:00 does not establish line movement.
quant_chain_flags: Independent Poisson underweights late incentive shifts, early goal, red card, and correlated transition events.
Full v3 factor_inputs and factor_weights are not exposed in the reviewed baseline.
No structured predicted_lineups means player-state and tactical assumptions cannot be fully audited.

recommended_probability_adjustment.suggestion: Before T-75 and final availability, keep Croatia win in a review band around 47-50% rather than publishing 50.4% as firm; preserve or slightly raise draw around 29-31% and Ghana counter/set-piece tail.

missing_data: final Croatia veteran minutes and rotation plan
line movement and official sale-status recheck
full v3 factor inputs and weights

handoff_notes.request: Confirm official XI, Ghana key availability, Croatia old-core minutes limits, cards and suspension risk.
Stress-test Ghana low block, transition threat, and Croatia late chase triggers under simultaneous-match score states.
Add v3 predicted_lineups, expose factor weights, audit duplicate counting, and thicken draw/late-transition tails.
Add same-source totals and a fresh 1X2/handicap snapshot with movement notes.

top_concerns.concern: Ordinary 1X2 is not available in the reviewed China Sports Lottery snapshot; Panama +2 handicap cannot replace full 1X2 calibration.
T-75 official lineups and final England rotation/minutes plan are missing; Rice, Saka, Reece James and other management decisions materially affect xG and tempo.
England's second-round 0-0 warns that control does not guarantee stable low-block chance conversion; the 68.0% win estimate needs a pre-lineup cap.
Panama have only a theoretical third-place route but can still alter tempo; England may manage top-two risk instead of chasing margin.

upset_or_failure_scenarios: England rotate and accept a low-tempo control match, extending 0-1, 0-0 and 1-1 corridors.
Panama start aggressively because only a theoretical third-place path remains, creating either early chaos or England transition chances.
A red card, penalty or early goal makes the +2 handicap signal unstable and separates England win from margin outcomes.
overconfidence_flags: England win at 68.0% is materially lineup-sensitive before T-75.
0-0 and 1-1 are both in or near the top-score cluster, so low-score tail must remain visible.
England win, England by two, and England by three-plus are different settlement layers and must not be merged.
market_flags: Panama +2 handicap snapshot implies deep England margin is not low-variance.
Single snapshot does not capture late movement around England rotation news.
quant_chain_flags: Poisson underweights early goals, red cards, favorite rotation, low-block tempo drag and score-state dependence.
The baseline does not expose full v3 factor_inputs, weights, predicted_lineups, or simulated_group_outcomes.
Market +2 signal was used to raise England xG from 1.58 to 1.75; this needs duplicate-counting control against favorite strength and Panama must-open factors.

recommended_probability_adjustment.suggestion: Before T-75, final availability and ordinary 1X2 are complete, review England win 68.0% in a 63-66% band; raise draw/low-score and Panama small-loss uncertainty while keeping England late-transition blowout as a scenario, not a stable baseline.

missing_data: final Panama lineup and Carrasquilla availability
line movement and official sale-status recheck
full v3 factor inputs and weights

handoff_notes.request: Confirm official XI, England rotation/minutes limits, Panama availability, cards and suspension risk.
Define England low-block breaking plan, Panama must-open triggers, and late-game transition state changes.
Cap England pre-lineup probability, split win/margin/score clusters, and audit the market +2 xG uplift for duplicate counting.
Add ordinary 1X2 and same-source totals; do not use Panama +2 as a substitute for full market calibration.

alternative_scenarios: Both matches kick off simultaneously, so the Croatia-Ghana state can change England's top-place risk appetite and vice versa.
If Ghana protect a draw and England also control risk, the group can drift into low-event corridors that punish favorite-margin assumptions.
If Croatia or Panama must chase late, transition and red-card tails become thicker than independent Poisson suggests.
follow_up_checks: Recheck official lineups, benches, formations and core rests after T-75.
Recheck final injuries, suspensions, yellow-card suspension risk, minutes limits and workload.
Add same-source ordinary 1X2, handicap and totals odds with capture time and sale status.
Create or update L group v3 prediction artifacts with predicted_lineups, factor_inputs, factor_weights and simulated_group_outcomes.
Audit duplicate counting between first two rounds, player_state, tactical adjustments, third-round motivation and market calibration.
publish_gate: hold until official lineups, final availability, complete odds chain, v3 predicted_lineups and red-team recheck are complete
